assertions.description: fetch() is exposed; the TCP pool change is invisible at the JS API surface but fetch must continue working.
XMLHttpRequest is exposed; legacy clients share the same TCP socket pool whose size is now under experiment.
PerformanceResourceTiming is exposed; entries surface connection reuse signals (connectStart/connectEnd) that are sensitive to pool sizing.
performance.getEntriesByType is available — authors observe pool effects by reading 'resource' entries.
When navigator.connection (Network Information API) is present, it exposes effectiveType — independent of pool sizing but a related platform-level connection signal.
The browser does not expose a JS-visible setter for the per-profile TCP pool size (the experiment is internal). globalThis must not contain a 'setMaxSockets' API.
